detail:
The reviewer's facts check out and the runtime symptom genuinely persists. send_gate_confirmation (messaging.py:311-351) is typed -> None and returns None in every branch; the no-surface branch (messaging.py:336-344) only emits a logger.warning. The caller views.py:172 invokes it without inspecting any outcome and always returns HTTP 202 {status:'pending', expires_at} (views.py:181-188). So an iOS-only App Store user (DeviceToken, no telegram_chat_id/line_user_id, preferred_channel defaulting to 'telegram') with GWS connected who triggers a destructive gated action (gmail_delete/calendar_delete/drive_delete/task_delete/gmail_send) still creates a PendingAction, the container polls GatePollView, and after 5 minutes is_expired (models.py:80-81) flips it to EXPIRED with no actionable prompt and no in-band failure surfaced to the container or user. The defect named exactly this silent-expiry symptom, and at the runtime level it is NOT resolved -- only the server-side log message changed (misleading 'no Telegram chat_id' -> accurate no-surface warning) and a doomed Telegram send was avoided.

It is genuine but narrow, and the disposition is honest. The fix correctly does what it claims within its constraints: routes channel resolution through resolve_user_channel via a lazy import (no circular import), keeps Telegram/LINE delivery unchanged, and silently fixes the prior 'preferred=line but only telegram linked' misroute; the 4 regression tests pass legitimately (model defaults '' match the assertions). The disposition is explicitly marked 'partial' and accurately documents the residual, correctly attributing it to cluster C33's single-file scope (only apps/actions/messaging.py): surfacing a failure to the container requires views.py, and the APNs/since-feed/app-respond path requires apps/router. The disposition also argues -- reasonably -- against firing a bare APNs alert the user could not act on. So this is not a wrongly-dismissed defect, not a regression, and not a broken fix; it is an honestly-scoped partial whose user-visible defect (silent gate expiry for iOS-only GWS users) remains live. I rate it genuine-incomplete at LOW severity: the runtime impact is real but the blast radius is tightly bounded (iOS-only + GWS connected + destructive gated action), the original defect's interim guard was 'surface a clear failure' which is only partially met (server log, not container/user), and the residual is openly tracked for the follow-up apps/router work.
suggested_fix: To fully satisfy the interim guard within the actions app: have send_gate_confirmation return a boolean (delivered/undelivered) and have views.py inspect it -- when no usable channel exists, return a response that the container can act on (e.g. status 'undeliverable' or an immediate non-pending result with a reason), instead of HTTP 202 'pending' that only ends in a silent 'expired' poll. The complete fix (tracked in the disposition) adds, in apps/router, a gate push + ?since= gate row for channel=='app' plus an iOS-callable gate-respond endpoint wrapping GateRespondView, then wires send_gate_confirmation to invoke that app path when resolve_user_channel returns 'app'.
evidence: apps/actions/messaging.py:311-351 (send_gate_confirmation returns None in all branches; no-surface case at 336-344 only logs a warning)
apps/actions/views.py:170-188 (caller ignores send_gate_confirmation outcome; always returns HTTP 202 'pending' + expires_at)
apps/actions/models.py:79-81 (is_expired flips PENDING->EXPIRED after timeout; unchanged for iOS-only users -> container gets 'expired' with no explanation)

docs/feature-audit/DEFECTS.json FA-0006 suggested_fix 'Minimum interim guard: ... do not leave the action to silently expire -- surface a clear failure back to the container/user' (container/user path unchanged; only server log changed)
docs/feature-audit/fix_dispositions.json FA-0006 action='partial' with noop_or_partial_reason documenting the residual apps/router + views.py work as out-of-scope for cluster C33
